## Metadata
- Repo: diffdoc
- File path: src/commands/init.ts
- File name: init.ts
- Extension: .ts
- Inferred language/type: TypeScript
- Content hash: bd438b2c62d4a904e020d7fb2120bd0c
- Line count: 278
- Byte size: 10094
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: runInit, buildInteractiveConfig, buildYesConfig, writeJsonFile, createIgnoreFile, updateGitignore
- External dependencies: fs (node:fs/promises), path (node:path), readline (node:readline/promises), process (node:process)
- Internal dependencies: SCHEMA_BASE_URL (from ../schemas)
- Public API/exports: runInit

## Purpose
This file is responsible for initializing the DiffDoc configuration by creating or updating configuration files based on user input or default settings.

## User-Visible Behavior
When executed, the `runInit` function prompts the user for configuration details if not provided, creates a configuration file, an ignore file, and updates the `.gitignore` file. It outputs a summary of created, updated, skipped files, and any warnings encountered during the process.

## Business Rules
- The configuration must specify an AI provider, which can be either "local" or "cloud".
- If a configuration file already exists and the `force` option is not set, the operation will skip writing that file and log a warning.
- The user can choose to store the OpenAI API key in the configuration file when using the cloud provider.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
- Inputs: Command-line options (e.g., `yes`, `provider`, `config`, `baseDir`, `force`), user prompts for configuration values.
- Outputs: JSON configuration file, ignore file, updates to `.gitignore`, and console output summarizing the changes made.

## Side Effects
- Creates or updates configuration files and directories on the filesystem.
- Modifies the `.gitignore` file to include paths relevant to the DiffDoc setup.
- Logs output to the console regarding the initialization process.

## Error And Edge Cases
- If the specified configuration file does not exist, it will be created.
- If invalid input is provided for the AI provider, an error will be thrown.
- If the user cancels the prompts or provides invalid data, defaults will be used where applicable.

## Dependencies
- External: Node.js built-in modules (`fs/promises`, `path`, `readline/promises`, `process`).
- Internal: `SCHEMA_BASE_URL` from the local schemas module.

## Operational Notes
- The script uses asynchronous file operations, which may affect performance if many files are being created or updated.
- The configuration process can be run in a non-interactive mode using the `yes` option, which uses defaults for all prompts.
- The script assumes the current working directory is the root of the repository for path resolutions.
